name: Cobbin
concept: A stubby, perpetually-damp river stone creature whose body carries one unbroken chalk-white luck-ring and a paler, glossier thumb-dimple on its belly, worn there by a thousand hands rubbing it for good fortune — and it is very proud of it.
imagePrompt:
Chunky pixel-art creature sprite, full body, standing, facing viewer, drawn on a coarse pixel grid with a limited 10-colour palette, hard clean pixel edges, no anti-aliasing except deliberate 2-step dithering.

SILHOUETTE: one single connected mass — a fat, water-worn river stone standing upright. Broad rounded egg shape, wider than it is tall, heavier at the bottom, flat-ish base so it sits with real weight. Two very short stubby legs fused directly into the body (each just a rounded pebble-toe block, no ankles), and two short rounded flipper-arms hanging at its sides like small smooth stones, tucked close to the body. No neck: the head IS the stone. The whole outline is soft, closed and instantly readable — a bean, a boulder, a thumb. Nothing sticks out thinner than a chunky pixel block; no strands, no hair, no floating pieces.

MATERIAL: smoothed river granite. Matte speckled stone rendered with coarse pixel dithering — scattered lighter grey and darker charcoal pixels like mineral flecks. One broad soft-edged highlight arc across the upper-left of the body reads as a permanent water sheen; a couple of blocky wet-gloss pixels along the top curve. The stone always looks slightly damp.

PALETTE: warm slate grey body (cool mid-grey with a hint of blue-brown), darker charcoal-plum outline, pale cool grey highlights. ONE bold colour: a soft mossy jade green — a small smooth cap of moss on top of the head, drawn as a rounded chunky blob with a clean scalloped lower edge, plus a couple of green flecks near the shoulder. Accent: chalk-cream white.

THE ONE IDEA: a single unbroken chalk-white band circles the entire body horizontally, chest-height, like a belt — a true "wishing stone" ring, thick, confident, hand-worn, slightly wobbly like nature drew it. And low on the belly, just under the ring, there is a shallow oval THUMB DIMPLE, polished paler and glossier than the rest of the stone, with a tight ring of lighter pixels around it: the lucky spot a thousand thumbs have rubbed smooth. Cobbin stands with one flipper-arm resting just beside the dimple, presenting it.

FACE: large, wide-set oval eyes with big black pupils and one single square white glint pixel each — honest, calm, unblinking, slightly proud. A tiny simple curved mouth, a small upturned smile, no teeth. Two soft blush patches of warm dusty rose on the cheeks. No eyebrows, no nose.

MOOD: heavy, warm, patient, quietly pleased with itself. A creature you would put in your pocket and keep for luck.
